q: The SI unit of magnetic flux is the _______.
A _______ is a device for generating alternating current.

A transformer that increases the voltage is called a _______ transformer.
A transformer that decreases the voltage is called a _______ transformer.
The SI unit of self-inductance is the _______.
Lenz's law is based on the law of conservation of _______.
The frequency of alternating current in Myanmar is _______.
In a DC, the _______ flow steadily in the same direction all the time.
The current delivered by a battery is a _______ current.
An electric _______ converts electrical energy into mechanical energy.
The magnetic flux Φ is equal to _______ cos θ.
Induced emf is proportional to the rate of change of _______.
Eddy currents cause energy loss in the form of _______.
The _______ emf in a motor opposes the applied voltage.
The transformer coil connected to an alternating voltage source is called the _______ coil.
The transformer coil connected to an electrical device is called the _______ coil.
The self-induced emf is also known as _______.
The unit of magnetic flux density is the _______.
One Weber is equal to one Tesla-metre _______.
Self-induction is sometimes called the _______ of electricity.
The current flowing in power lines is _______ current.
Mutual induction occurs between _______ coils.
In an _______ transformer, the efficiency is 100%.
The ignition system of motor cars uses an _______ coil.
The voltage used in Myanmar is between 220 V and _______.
A _______ is used to measure very small current.
AC generators work on _______ law of electromagnetic induction.
A generator converts _______ energy into electrical energy.
If the magnetic field is perpendicular to the surface, the magnetic flux is _______.
If the magnetic field is parallel to the surface, the magnetic flux is _______.
The _______ current changes its direction periodically.
The secondary wavelets have the same _______ as the primary wavelets.
The secondary wavelets have the same _______ as the primary wavelets.
The efficiency of a transformer is the ratio of output power to _______ power.
The self-inductance L is given by the formula L = _______ / I.
The _______ core of a transformer is used to provide a path for magnetic flux.
One _______ is equivalent to one Weber per Ampere.
Transformers can only work with _______ current.

The _______ of the core reduces the energy loss due to eddy currents.
The ignition system sparking occurs between the two terminals of the _______.
In generators, mechanical energy is used to rotate the _______.
The alternating current induced in a rotating coil varies _______ with time.
The transformer ratio is given by Vs / Vp = _______ / Np.
The emf is induced only when there is a _______ in magnetic flux.
A _______ uses direct current from a battery.
